canonical_deployment.notes: Canonical openjanus deployment. SDK + ai-tools reference this address.

smoke_deployment.purpose: Used by scripts/smoke-janus-ft.mjs because the stub babyAddStub helper overflows once commitments accumulate across runs. Smoke needs a fresh-state account that can be reset via Admin.resetCommitmentsForTestingOnly() between runs.

underlying_default.note: Testnet FlowToken.Vault — configurable via Admin.setUnderlyingVaultType() to any FungibleToken vault type identifier

deploy_tx_hashes.canonical_initial_deploy: n/a (flow CLI add-contract did not surface tx id; openjanus-flow sequence number bumped to 66 around 2026-05-27 07:31 UTC)
deploy_tx_hashes.canonical_update_post_admin_reset: n/a (flow CLI update-contract; both accounts updated to add Admin.resetCommitmentsForTestingOnly)

lab_provenance.spike_deployment: 0x7599043aea001283 (testnet-claucondor; lab-only, do not use for prod)
lab_provenance.diff_from_lab: Added Admin.resetCommitmentsForTestingOnly() so the same JanusFT instance can be smoke-tested repeatedly without contract removal (Flow testnet does not authorize contract removal).

limitations.crypto: babyAddStub + babyNegateStub are NOT cryptographically valid BabyJubJub point ops — they are placeholders so the lab can validate event/storage/calldata PRIVACY SHAPE without depending on cross-VM proof verification. With real Pedersen-shaped coordinates (each near the curve order p), accumulated stub state OVERFLOWS UInt256 after a couple of operations. Real soundness arrives in v0.5 when cross-VM verification via the EVM ConfidentialTransferVerifier lands and the stub helpers are replaced with COA calls to BabyJub.sol.
limitations.proof_verification: amountProofBytes + proofBytes are NOT verified on-chain in this version — only their length > 0 is checked. The privacy properties under test are STRUCTURAL (what data appears on calldata, storage, events), independent of soundness.
limitations.registry_model: The CommitmentRegistry MUST be on the signer's account (spike model — wrap_ft/unwrap_ft borrow it from /storage/janusFTRegistry on the signer). Multiple distinct accounts CAN hold commitments via shieldedTransfer (recipient just receives a commitment, no registry required on their side). The wrapper/unwrapper account is the registry holder.
limitations.unwrap_unverifiable: Unwrap is INTENTIONALLY SKIPPED by scripts/smoke-janus-ft.mjs — it deterministically overflows the stub babyAddStub during totalSupplyCommitment debit. The lab spike (cadence-crypto-lab/.../multi-user-stress-ft.json#steps.unwrap) skipped it for the same reason. Real unwrap works end-to-end on the EVM side (see openjanus-contracts/packages/janus-token/deployments/cross-vm-smoke-results.json step 4).

next_steps_v0_5: Replace babyAdd/babyNegateStub with cross-VM calls to BabyJub.sol via COA
Replace amountProofBytes opaque accept with cross-VM call to ConfidentialTransferVerifier
Remove resetCommitmentsForTestingOnly admin once real BabyJub homomorphic state works
Provide an alternative registry model where the registry lives on the deployer account and users interact via capabilities (multi-user wraps without owning a registry resource)
